mapper.select:
SELECT ps.id AS problem_set_id, ps.title, COALESCE(ps.description, '') AS description, ps.is_public, COALESCE(pc.problem_count, 0) AS problem_count, CASE WHEN #{isAdmin} THEN 1 WHEN #{userId} IS NOT NULL AND EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM resource_permission rp WHERE rp.resource_type = 'PROBLEM_SET' AND rp.resource_id = ps.id AND rp.user_id = #{userId} AND rp.permission = 'WRITE' AND rp.is_del = 0 ) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS can_write FROM problem_set ps LEFT JOIN ( SELECT pspr.problem_set_id, COUNT(1) AS problem_count FROM problem_set_problem_rel pspr INNER JOIN problem p ON p.id = pspr.problem_id WHERE pspr.is_del = 0 AND p.is_del = 0 GROUP BY pspr.problem_set_id ) pc ON pc.problem_set_id = ps.id WHERE ps.is_del = 0 AND (
1 = 1
ps.is_public = 1
OR EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM resource_permission rp WHERE rp.resource_type = 'PROBLEM_SET' AND rp.resource_id = ps.id AND rp.user_id = #{userId} AND rp.is_del = 0 )
) ORDER BY ps.id DESC

SELECT p.pid, p.title, pspr.sort_order FROM problem_set_problem_rel pspr INNER JOIN problem p ON p.id = pspr.problem_id WHERE pspr.problem_set_id = #{problemSetId} AND pspr.is_del = 0 AND p.is_del = 0 ORDER BY pspr.sort_order ASC, p.id ASC
